general: Affected by the "Zi-Wu clash," those born in the Year of the Rat will face the clash with Tai Sui in the Year of the Horse, making them one of the most significantly affected among the twelve zodiac signs, requiring careful handling. While the fortune brought by the clash with Tai Sui isn't necessarily entirely negative, it can be polarized, especially in the Bing-Wu year when the fire element is strong. Those whose birth chart favors fire may make some progress, but those who are averse to fire will face numerous challenges. The saying goes, "One joy can ward off three disasters, without joy comes trouble." In a year of clashing with Tai Sui, it's advisable to hold celebratory events such as weddings, the birth of a child, purchasing real estate, or starting a business to mitigate the negative impact of the clash. However, those without joyous events should prepare themselves for changes in their career, relationships, or family. It's recommended to lower expectations, avoid making major decisions, and adopt a "letting things take their course" attitude throughout the year. Although the fortune brought by the clash with Tai Sui is unstable, fortunately, the auspicious star "Tang Fu" shines brightly, providing support for the careers of those born in the Year of the Rat. This star symbolizes power and prestige. Those working in disciplined forces such as the police, customs, or fire department are expected to demonstrate leadership abilities in the Year of the Horse. Those in large organizations, government units, or management departments will also be able to fully utilize their talents. The star "Heavenly Chef," as its name suggests, is related to food and symbolizes active social life in the Year of the Horse, indicating many opportunities for gatherings, social events, and enjoying fine dining. However, the new year also brings the inauspicious star "Year Breaker," signifying a breakdown in interpersonal relationships, especially the potential to offend those of high status. Those born in the Year of the Rat must be mindful of their words and actions to avoid misunderstandings and attacks. "Great Loss" represents financial loss; in the Year of the Horse, poor judgment can easily lead to financial losses. Therefore, in both investment and entrepreneurship, it is advisable to take small risks and avoid high-risk speculative transactions. "Disaster Star" and "Heavenly Cry" symbolize unexpected situations during travel. It is recommended to purchase medical and travel insurance in advance to prevent flight delays or lost luggage. Avoid participating in high-risk outdoor activities; even if you insist on participating, seek the accompaniment of a professional instructor. Safety is always paramount. In reality, clashing with Tai Sui is like being attacked from the outside world; remaining still makes you vulnerable to injury. Therefore, it is advisable to move around more to avoid danger and seek help from others for protection. Those born under the Rat, Monkey, and Dragon signs, who form a harmonious triad, should wear monkey or dragon zodiac ornaments in the Year of the Horse to enhance their energy. It is also best to have a health check-up at the end of the Year of the Snake and worship Tai Sui again after the start of spring for health management and medical assistance. Furthermore, avoid contact with the Five Yellow (South) and Two Black (Northwest) stars, which are associated with illness in the year. With adequate preparation, even a year of clash can bring peace and good fortune.
career: During the Year of the Horse, those born in the Year of the Rat will be blessed with the auspicious star "Tang Fu," symbolizing authority and prestige. Their careers will be smooth, and their work performance will be vibrant. Those working in disciplinary agencies such as the police, customs, and fire departments will receive increased support and have opportunities to showcase their leadership skills, guiding subordinates to achieve outstanding results. Furthermore, those in management positions or large organizations will also benefit, expecting to shine in the workplace, gain the appreciation of their superiors, and increase their chances of promotion. However, due to the unfavorable interpersonal relationships caused by "Sui Po," personnel disputes will be frequent during the Year of the Horse. It is important to remain humble and low-key in interactions to avoid offending important figures through unintentional words, which could negatively impact the otherwise positive development of one's career. Additionally, some born in the Year of the Rat may consider changing jobs during this conflicting year; however, with fluctuating fortunes, the risks must be carefully assessed. If you insist on leaving, it's advisable to do so in the second half of the year, and if necessary, sign a contract confirming your resignation from your current position. Avoid impulsive resignations without a backup plan, as changes at the new company could lead to a prolonged waiting period or a work environment that doesn't meet your expectations, leaving you in a dilemma. In fact, during a year of conflict with Tai Sui, if you feel your career development is hindered and want to make changes, you can consider applying for business trips, transfers, or overseas assignments within your current position. At the same time, adopting a "letting go" mentality, lowering your goals slightly, and enrolling in further education courses to equip yourself will make changes more appropriate when the time is right.
financial: In terms of wealth this year, those born in the Year of the Rat will face significant fluctuations. For those in the workforce, the auspicious star "Tang Fu" provides support, especially during the Year of the Horse, potentially leading to a reasonable salary adjustment. However, for business people, their performance will show marked ups and downs across different months, experiencing periods of extreme wealth and poverty. This necessitates a greater focus on increasing revenue, reducing expenditure, and stockpiling resources for unforeseen circumstances. Especially given the unfavorable global economic situation, it's advisable to avoid lending or extending credit even to familiar clients, as this could lead to liquidity problems and inability to repay, or losses due to external factors. In fact, in years of conflict, action is often more beneficial than inaction. The Year of the Horse is suitable for expanding products and services to other countries and regions, as "money comes from movement" will bring unexpected business opportunities. However, the Year of the Horse still carries potential risks to wealth. Coupled with the influence of the inauspicious star "Da Hao," both starting a business and investing require extra caution. Even seemingly easy projects should be thoroughly prepared in advance to avoid discrepancies between expectations and reality, or unexpected changes that could catch you off guard. It is recommended that investment strategies for the new year be based on stability, avoiding excessive investment. Small investments are preferable to aim for higher returns, thus increasing the chances of success. Due to the volatile nature of wealth in this year of conflict, those born in the Year of the Rat should not set overly high goals. After receiving income, a portion of the cash can be used to purchase tangible assets or real estate to preserve value. Remember that these assets should be used for personal use, not for speculation. Funds can also be used for relocation, renovation, repairs, and health management, proactively adapting to the changing fortunes brought about by the clash with Tai Sui.

Due to the strong clash with the zodiac sign, those born in the Year of the Rat will experience significant pressure. If the main entrance or the ancestral tablet is located in the position of the Five Yellow (South) or Two Black (Northwest) illness stars of the year, heavy bronze objects should be placed there to neutralize the negative influence. The upcoming new year is also a year prone to happy events; if you intend to add a family member, you should finalize the plans as soon as possible. bornYear.reading: In the new year, your financial and social fortunes are promising. If you wish to improve your health, consider minor renovations, maintenance, or replacement of broken appliances in your living space. Remember to avoid construction in areas affected by the Five Yellow (South) and Two Black (Northwest) illness stars to prevent the worsening of health problems.
